# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 417
- **Lines of code**: 101,081
- **Languages**: TypeScript (335), JSON (25), CSS (17), Other (13), JavaScript (12)
- **Zones**: 12 (12 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-07T21:07:31.375Z

## Architecture Zones

### Agent Core System (`agent-core`)

Core agent functionality including CLI loops, prompts, and token management with high internal coupling.

- Files: 22
- Cohesion: 0.73, Coupling: 0.27
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/brief.ts, packages/hench/src/agent/cli-loop.ts, packages/hench/src/agent/prompt.ts, packages/hench/src/agent/stuck.ts, packages/hench/src/agent/summary.ts (+4 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/agent-core/context.md`

### Agent Tools Layer (`agent-tools-layer`)

Core tool implementations and safety guards that provide controlled access to system operations for AI agents.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.69, Coupling: 0.31
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/tools.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/dispatch.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/files.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/shell.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/agent-tools-layer/context.md`

### Application Entry Points (`application-entry`)

Top-level application configuration and entry points for different execution modes.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/application-entry/context.md`

### Application Orchestration (`application-orchestration`)

Main application logic coordinating CLI commands, agent loops, storage, and system configuration.

- Files: 29
- Cohesion: 0.78, Coupling: 0.22
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/loop.ts, packages/hench/src/agent/review.ts, packages/hench/src/cli/commands/run.ts, packages/hench/src/store/config.ts, packages/hench/src/store/index.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/application-orchestration/context.md`

### Claude API Client (`claude-api-client`)

Dedicated package for Claude API integration with provider abstraction and token management.

- Files: 13
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-api-client/context.md`

### Completion Validation (`completion-validation`)

Specialized module for AI completion processing, validation, and regex-based text operations.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 0.74, Coupling: 0.26
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts, packages/hench/src/validation/completion.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/completion-validation/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

### Specialized Tools (`specialized-tools`)

Specific tool implementations for development operations including shell execution, git operations, and test running.

- Files: 6
- Cohesion: 0.56, Coupling: 0.44
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/tools/exec-shell.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/git.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/specialized-tools/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/login → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_auth.login.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Patterns

- Security-first architecture with guard mechanisms controlling tool access creates proper safety boundaries [info] (agent-tools-layer)
- Entry zone maintains clean downstream dependency flow with zero coupling to other zones [info] (application-entry)
- Demonstrates ideal package encapsulation pattern that could be replicated across hench packages [info] (claude-api-client)
- Rex tool serving 3 zones indicates successful shared utility extraction that could be model for other cross-cutting concerns [info] (completion-validation)
- Architecture exhibits classic layered pattern with proper top-down dependency flow, but middle layers show concerning bidirectional coupling [info]
- Security boundary pattern emerges with guard mechanisms providing controlled access layer between AI agents and system operations [info]
- Incomplete test coverage for high-risk shell execution tools creates potential reliability issues [warning] (specialized-tools)

### Relationships

- Acts as architectural hub but bidirectional coupling with application-orchestration creates circular dependency risk [warning] (agent-core)
- High import volume (20+ from agent-core) combined with 29 files indicates potential architectural antipattern of god zone [warning] (application-orchestration)
- Hench package zones collectively show 53 cross-imports while claude package maintains zero, indicating inconsistent architectural discipline [warning]
- Tool zones demonstrate proper dependency inversion with specialized implementations depending on abstract agent layer [info]

### Observations

- 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (agent-core)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (application-entry)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-api-client)
- Bidirectional coupling: "agent-core" ↔ "application-orchestration" (11+20 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "agent-tools-layer" ↔ "application-orchestration" (2+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "agent-tools-layer" ↔ "specialized-tools" (2+3 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 29 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Heavy cross-zone import activity (31 total imports with hench-3) indicates tight coupling with main application logic [warning] (agent-core)
- High coupling (0.27) with 9 entry points indicates potential over-fragmentation of responsibilities [warning] (agent-core)
- ... and 25 more
